JT-001.body:
Title: JT-001 - Create private GitHub repository

Mini-PRD / Context:
Create `job-search-intelligence` under the personal account and keep it private.
This ticket contributes to the local-first job-search intelligence app by preserving the `applications` table and event timeline as the factual source of truth.

Linked requirements:
FR-0, FR-6, NFR-5, NFR-8, Phase 0

Scope - in:
- Deliver the specific behavior described by this ticket.
- Keep the change atomic enough for one focused implementation session.
- Touch the planned modules only unless implementation discovery shows a documented need.

Scope - out:
- Do not introduce telemetry, shared credentials, autonomous outbound email, auto-apply behavior, or multi-user SaaS assumptions.
- Do not add broad abstractions outside the architecture contract.
- Do not move later-phase work into this ticket unless explicitly listed.

Technical approach:
- Follow Repository pattern for data access, Strategy for email and LLM providers, Pipeline stages passing Pydantic DTOs, service layer business logic, FastAPI dependency injection, typed errors, mypy-clean code, and ruff formatting.
- Keep API routes thin and put business behavior in services.
- Ensure secrets are encrypted at rest and never logged.
- The LLM never emits raw SQL for execution; use deterministic SQL, typed Python logic, or constrained query builders as appropriate.
- Planned files/modules: backend/app/api/, docs/, tickets/.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The ticket behavior is implemented and mapped to the linked requirements.
- [ ] The implementation preserves local-first SQLite storage and the `applications` plus `application_events` invariant.
- [ ] Relevant DTOs, repositories, services, and API boundaries are typed.
- [ ] Relevant tests, evals, or smoke checks are added or updated.
- [ ] User-facing behavior is visible in the app, API, docs, or generated backlog artifact as appropriate.

Dependencies:
- Blocked by: None.
- Blocks: None listed in this generated manifest.

Definition of Done:
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are met.
- [ ] Code or documentation is merged into the branch.
- [ ] Architecture and privacy conventions are honored.
- [ ] Verification commands relevant to the touched area have been run and recorded.
- [ ] For classification-related changes, the golden-set eval passes if applicable.

Estimate: M
